sections.heading: Data subjects are provided with channels to enquire about, submit appeals for and
sections.title: Data subjects must have channels to appeal AIDA decisions
sections.summary: Data subjects must be provided with channels to inquire about, submit appeals for, and request reviews of AIDA-driven decisions that affect them. This ensures data subjects can seek clarification or correction of decisions made by AI systems, maintaining transparency and accountability in the decision-making process.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires data subject appeal mechanisms, critical for ensuring compliance with data subject rights in cross-border data transfers involving AI.

requirements.text: Data subjects must have channels to inquire about, submit appeals for, and request reviews of AIDA-driven decisions

sections.source_quote: 10. Data subjects are provided with channels to enquire about, submit appeals for and request reviews of AIDA-driven decisions that affect them.
sections.relevance_reason: Data subject appeal channels for AI decisions

sections.heading: Verified and relevant supplementary data provided by data subjects are taken into
sections.title: Verified supplementary data from data subjects must be considered
sections.summary: When reviewing AIDA-driven decisions, firms must take into account verified and relevant supplementary data provided by data subjects. This allows data subjects to update or correct information for accuracy and relevance, ensuring decisions are based on the most current and reliable data.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires incorporation of data subject-provided corrections, important for maintaining data accuracy in cross-border AI-driven decision-making.

requirements.text: Verified and relevant supplementary data provided by data subjects must be taken into account during AIDA decision reviews

sections.source_quote: 11. Verified and relevant supplementary data provided by data subjects are taken into account when performing a review of AIDA-driven decisions.
sections.relevance_reason: Data subject rights to correct information

sections.heading: To increase public confidence, use of AIDA is proactively disclosed to data subjects as
sections.title: AIDA usage disclosed to data subjects for transparency
sections.summary: Entities must proactively disclose to data subjects the use of AIDA systems as part of their communication to build public confidence. This transparency measure ensures data subjects are informed about how automated decision-making systems are applied in their financial interactions.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates disclosure of AIDA usage to data subjects, which impacts DTIAs by requiring transparency in how automated decisions are communicated to individuals.

requirements.text: Use of AIDA is proactively disclosed to data subjects as part of general communication

sections.source_quote: To increase public confidence, use of AIDA is proactively disclosed to data subjects as part of general communication.

sections.heading: Data subjects are provided, upon request, clear explanations on what data is used to
sections.title: Data subjects must receive clear explanations of data used in AIDA decisions
sections.summary: Data subjects must be provided with clear explanations upon request about what data is used to make AIDA-driven decisions and how that data affects the decision. This ensures transparency without exposing proprietary information, allowing data subjects to understand the decision-making process.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires transparent explanations of data usage, critical for assessing data transfer compliance with data subject rights in cross-border contexts.

requirements.text: Data subjects must receive clear explanations upon request about data used in AIDA decisions and how it affects the decision

sections.source_quote: 13. Data subjects are provided, upon request, clear explanations on what data is used to make AIDA-driven decisions about the data subject and how the data affects the decision.
sections.relevance_reason: Data subject transparency in AIDA decisions

sections.heading: Data subjects are provided, upon request, clear explanations on the consequences
sections.title: Explanations for AIDA-driven decisions
sections.summary: This section mandates that data subjects receive clear explanations of how AIDA-driven decisions affect them when they request it. The explanations must cover the consequences of specific decisions, such as premium adjustments for insurance. The requirements include providing illustrative materials, user interfaces for input variation, and sharing relevant data used in analysis. These obligations apply to AIDA firms operating in Singapore's financial sector.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires transparency in AIDA-driven decisions, mandating that financial institutions provide clear explanations to customers about the impact of AI decisions on their outcomes. This directly affects data transfer impact assessments by requiring explanations for decisions made using AI models, especially when data is processed across borders.

requirements.text: Data subjects must receive clear explanations of consequences of AIDA-driven decisions upon request
Firms must provide illustrative diagrams and charts showing how driving patterns affect premiums
Firms must develop user interfaces for customers to vary inputs and see premium impact
Firms must share which customer group a data subject falls into and corresponding premium impact
Firms must disclose data used to analyze driving patterns and allow updates
Firms must provide meaningful explanations for premium increases upon request

sections.source_quote: 14. Data subjects are provided, upon request, clear explanations on the consequences that AIDA-driven decisions may have on them.
sections.relevance_reason: AIDA-driven decisions affect financial data compliance directly

sections.heading: Use of personal attributes as input factors for AIDA-driven decisions is
sections.relevance_reason: AIDA decisions require data privacy compliance

sections.heading: AIDA-driven decisions are regularly reviewed so that models behave as
sections.title: Regular review of AIDA models to ensure intended behavior
sections.summary: AIDA-driven decisions must undergo regular reviews to ensure they function as designed and intended. The review frequency depends on the decision's materiality and model complexity, with more frequent checks required for highly material and complex models. These reviews help identify and correct errors or unintended biases in the decision-making process.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires regular model validation to ensure AIDA decisions align with intended behavior, critical for assessing model reliability in cross-border data transfers involving AI.

requirements.text: AIDA-driven decisions must be regularly reviewed to ensure they behave as designed and intended
Review frequency depends on decision materiality and model complexity
More frequent validation required for highly material and complex models
Quarterly, annual, or less frequent validations sufficient for less material and straightforward models

sections.source_quote: 4. AIDA-driven decisions are regularly reviewed so that models behave as designed and intended.
sections.relevance_reason: AIDA model reviews ensure compliance with data protection and bias mitigation

sections.heading: AIDA-driven decisions are held to at least the same ethical standards as
sections.title: AIDA decisions must meet same ethical standards as human decisions
sections.summary: AIDA-driven decisions must adhere to at least the same ethical standards as human-driven decisions. Firms must ensure their algorithms do not violate existing ethical principles, such as fairness and non-discrimination, by excluding protected attributes like gender in hiring decisions. This requires internal governance frameworks to maintain ethical alignment throughout the decision-making process.
sections.dtia_summary: Ensures AIDA decisions comply with ethical standards, reducing risks of biased outcomes in cross-border data transfers involving sensitive personal information.

requirements.text: AIDA-driven decisions must be held to at least the same ethical standards as human-driven decisions
Model or algorithm must not use gender as an input or derived attribute in hiring decisions
Firms must establish ethical review boards to oversee ethical standards

sections.source_quote: 6. AIDA-driven decisions are held to at least the same ethical standards as human driven decisions.
sections.relevance_reason: Ethical standards for algorithmic decisions directly impact data compliance

sections.heading: Firms using AIDA proactively raise management and Board awareness of their
sections.title: Firms must raise management and Board awareness of AIDA use
sections.summary: Firms using AIDA must proactively inform management and the Board about their AIDA usage. This includes setting approval authorities based on decision materiality and complexity, with higher-level approvals for significant decisions. Regular updates to the Board ensure oversight of material AIDA-driven decisions and their impacts.
sections.dtia_summary: Ensures board-level oversight of AIDA usage, critical for assessing risks in cross-border data transfers involving AI-driven decisions.

requirements.text: Firms must proactively raise management and Board awareness of AIDA use
Approval authorities must consider decision materiality and complexity
Higher-level approvals (e.g., CEO or Board) required for highly material decisions

sections.source_quote: 9. Firms using AIDA proactively raise management and Board awareness of their use of AIDA.
sections.relevance_reason: Board oversight of AIDA use for data compliance
